home.welcome: I build with AI and write about what works.

home.description: Product thinking, AI-native systems, and lessons from the trenches. Real notes from a builder who ships daily.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: I build with AI and write about what works.
about.bio1: I'm Aaron — a product builder and engineer at a financial firm. I lead product teams by day and write code by night. I've always been the person who wants to understand how things work, then build something better.
about.bio2: Right now I'm deep in the AI-native world — building execution systems, automating workflows, and figuring out what actually works vs. what's just hype. This site is where I share those lessons.
about.roleItem1: Product Leader at a Financial Firm
about.now1: Building AI-native execution systems — tools that help product and engineering teams ship faster without cutting corners.
about.now2: Exploring how to redesign daily life with AI — balancing family, health, career, and personal projects.
about.aboutMe: I'm Aaron — a product builder and engineer at a financial firm. I lead product teams by day and write code by night. This site is where I share real notes on product execution, AI-native systems, and building in public.

build.label: BUILD
build.title: Builder with rhythm. Human by design.
build.description: I lead products, still write code, and use AI to make more room for the life worth living.

think.label: THINK
think.title: Speed still needs judgment.
think.description: AI can accelerate the work. Choosing what matters, asking better questions, and knowing when to stop still belong to people.

play.label: PLAY
play.title: Leave room for rhythm.
play.description: Family, music, health, and time away from screens are not detours. They keep the work grounded and the thinking clear.

ways.title: Different rhythms. Same intention.
ways.intro: Build things that matter, then share what the work teaches.

build.title: Build — useful systems
build.description: I turn ambiguous problems into practical products, workflows, and tools.

write.title: Write — durable thinking
write.description: I share field notes from real experiments, including what failed and what changed my mind.

play.title: Play — drums & experiments
play.description: Music keeps me present. Experiments keep me curious.

live.title: Live — family, health, wonder
live.description: Life beyond the screen keeps the work honest and the judgment clear.

humanFirst.quote: The point of leverage is not to become a machine. It is to make life bigger.
humanFirst.description: AI-native systems remove friction. The time and attention they return are for what only humans can do — care, creativity, judgment, and deep relationships.

building.title: Building
building.description: Practical AI-native systems that turn intent into repeatable, useful work.

writing.title: Writing
writing.description: Field notes on product, execution, and the ideas that survive real use.

makingRoom.title: Making room
makingRoom.description: Time for family, health, music, and the quiet that clear thinking requires.

road.title: Between the problem and the system
road.body1: Much of my work happens between a messy real-world problem and the system that eventually makes it simpler.
road.body2: AI has widened what I can explore, but it has not changed my standard: useful, understandable, and human. This site is my field notebook for that road.

connect.title: If our paths overlap, say hello.
connect.description: I’m always glad to compare notes with thoughtful builders, operators, and curious people.

aiNative.title: AI Native
aiNative.description: Exploring how AI transforms my daily life.
aiNative.intro: This page is a playful showcase of the ways I'm weaving AI into my workflow and creativity.
aiNative.daily1: ChatGPT drafts my ideas and answers random questions.
aiNative.daily2: GitHub Copilot helps finish code faster.
aiNative.vision: Eventually it'll become my personal branding hub for everything AI – tutorials, projects, and experiences.

subscribe.rssExplanation: RSS (Really Simple Syndication) is an open standard for subscribing to content. Instead of checking websites manually, your RSS reader automatically fetches new posts from all your favorite sites in one place.
subscribe.rssBenefit1: No email clutter - read in your dedicated RSS reader
subscribe.rssBenefit2: No tracking or analytics - your privacy is protected
subscribe.rssBenefit3: Own your subscription - not controlled by any platform
subscribe.rssBenefit4: Works offline - download posts to read later

start.title: About Me
start.description: Builder, product leader, engineer. I ship with AI daily.
start.subtitle: I build with AI and write about what works. Product thinking, AI-native systems, and lessons from the trenches.
start.bio1: I'm Aaron — a product builder and engineer at a financial firm. I lead product teams by day and write code by night. I've always been the person who wants to understand how things work, then build something better.
start.bio2: Right now I'm deep in the AI-native world — building execution systems, automating workflows, and figuring out what actually works vs. what's just hype. This site is where I share those lessons.
start.now1: Building AI-native execution systems — tools that help product and engineering teams ship faster without cutting corners.
start.now2: Exploring how to redesign daily life with AI — balancing family, health, career, and personal projects.
start.path1Description: How to ship products that work. Strategy, alignment, and building teams that deliver.
start.path2Description: Real examples of AI solving real problems. Not theory — working systems.
start.path3Description: Honest notes from the journey. What worked, what didn't, what I'd do differently.

playbooks.newsletterTitle: Get notified when new playbooks are published
playbooks.newsletterDescription: Twice a month: one playbook, one lesson, one tool.

signal.title: Signal
signal.subtitle: Noise removed. What's left is what matters.
signal.researchDesk: Public research desk for AI-native work.
signal.researchDeskDescription: Signal tracks the public sources I use to understand coding agents, AI-native workflows, small-team automation, and product opportunities. Raw links are evidence; threads and briefs are my interpretation.
signal.metricSignalsCaption: Raw public evidence in the current snapshot.
signal.metricThreadsCaption: Active theses tested against the feed.
signal.metricBriefsCaption: Editorial memos pulled from repeated signals.
signal.heroPromiseEvidence: Raw links stay visible as evidence.
signal.heroPromiseInterpretation: Threads turn repeated signals into working theses.
signal.heroPromiseProducts: Briefs convert research into product hypotheses.
signal.previewDescription: Daily signals from my AI-native product research desk.
signal.watchingDescription: The research threads I am actively testing against the feed. Each thread turns repeated signals into a working thesis, an open question, and a possible product direction.
signal.question: Question
signal.briefsDescription: Short research notes that turn repeated signals into a sharper thesis. These live inside Signal, separate from the main Writing archive.
signal.poweredBy: Signal is my public research desk: selected public sources, working theses, and the product questions they raise.
signal.whatIsThisDesc: Signal follows focused technology topics, keeps the underlying sources inspectable, and separates research output from the main Writing archive.
signal.pulseDescription: The daily pulse is the desk's short read on what the current feed is saying.
signal.evidenceDescription: Search and filter the underlying links that support the daily pulse, research threads, and briefs.
signal.pulse: AI safety and the OpenAI vs Anthropic debate dominate today, while MCP protocol adoption accelerates globally.
signal.description: A public research desk for AI and technology. Structured evidence, working theses, and editorial notes.
signal.convergingEvidence: Evidence converging on a working thesis
signal.evidenceBehindFocusDescription: The five sources selected by the current Signal read. Open the source, inspect the synthesis, then decide whether the thesis holds.
signal.horizonDescription: Start with the current focus, then open only the thread you want to examine. The horizon is editorial, not an automated trend score.
signal.previousBriefingsDescription: Longer editorial notes stay folded until you want the full argument.
signal.workbenchDescription: Search the public evidence set by the dimensions that actually exist in the data. Corpus-wide source totals remain separate from the currently browseable results.
